description: 20-document corpus for engineering role BM25F regression gate

documents.title: Cargo Workspace Management
documents.body: Cargo workspaces allow multiple Rust crates to share a single Cargo.lock and build output directory. Use workspace resolver v2 for feature unification.
documents.description: Managing multi-crate Rust projects with Cargo workspaces

documents.title: CI/CD Pipeline Design
documents.body: Continuous integration pipelines automate build test and deployment workflows. GitHub Actions and GitLab CI are popular choices for Rust projects.
documents.description: Automated build and deployment pipeline patterns

documents.title: API Design with OpenAPI
documents.body: OpenAPI specifications define RESTful API contracts. Code generation from OpenAPI schemas accelerates client and server development.
documents.description: Designing APIs with OpenAPI and REST conventions

documents.title: Rust Trait System
documents.body: Traits define shared behaviour in Rust. Generic trait bounds enable zero-cost abstractions. Object safety constraints govern dynamic dispatch.
documents.description: Advanced use of Rust traits and generics

documents.title: Testing Strategies for Rust
documents.body: Unit tests live alongside source code in Rust. Integration tests reside in a tests directory. Property-based testing with proptest generates random inputs.
documents.description: Comprehensive testing approaches for Rust projects

documents.title: WebAssembly with Rust
documents.body: Rust compiles to WebAssembly via wasm-pack. WASM modules run in browsers Node.js and edge runtimes. wasm-bindgen bridges Rust and JavaScript types.
documents.description: Building WebAssembly modules with Rust and wasm-pack

documents.title: Async Runtime Internals
documents.body: Tokio implements a multi-threaded work-stealing scheduler. Tasks are lightweight green threads. IO is handled via epoll on Linux and kqueue on macOS.
documents.description: Deep dive into asynchronous runtime implementation

documents.title: Code Review Best Practices
documents.body: Effective code review focuses on correctness security and maintainability. Pull requests should be small and focused. Use conventional commits for clarity.
documents.description: Guidelines for effective engineering code reviews

documents.title: Performance Profiling in Rust
documents.body: Flame graphs identify CPU hotspots. Criterion benchmarks measure function performance. perf and cargo flamegraph are standard profiling tools.
documents.description: Tools and techniques for profiling Rust application performance

documents.title: Database Schema Evolution
documents.body: Schema migrations must be backward compatible during rolling deployments. SQLx compile-time query verification prevents runtime SQL errors.
documents.description: Safe database schema migration strategies

documents.title: Distributed Tracing with OpenTelemetry
documents.body: OpenTelemetry provides vendor-neutral tracing metrics and logging. Rust supports OTEL via the opentelemetry-rust crate. Jaeger stores and queries traces.
documents.description: Implementing distributed tracing for microservices

documents.title: Container Security Hardening
documents.body: Minimal container images reduce attack surface. Distroless images contain only the application binary. Non-root execution prevents privilege escalation.
documents.description: Security best practices for containerised applications

documents.title: Feature Flag Systems
documents.body: Feature flags decouple deployment from release. LaunchDarkly and Unleash provide flag management. Rust conditional compilation uses cfg attributes.
documents.description: Implementing and managing feature flags in production

documents.title: Static Analysis Tools for Rust
documents.body: Clippy provides idiomatic Rust linting. cargo-deny checks licence compatibility and security advisories. cargo-audit scans for known vulnerabilities.
documents.description: Static analysis and security scanning for Rust codebases

documents.title: Event-Driven Architecture
documents.body: Event streaming systems like Kafka Fluvio and NATS decouple producers from consumers. Consumer groups enable parallel processing of event streams.
documents.description: Patterns for event-driven and message-driven architectures

documents.title: Infrastructure as Code
documents.body: Terraform and Pulumi provision cloud infrastructure declaratively. GitOps workflows store infrastructure state in version control repositories.
documents.description: Managing infrastructure with code and version control

documents.title: Rust Lifetime Annotations
documents.body: Lifetime annotations prevent use-after-free and dangling references. The borrow checker enforces lifetime constraints at compile time.
documents.description: Understanding and applying Rust lifetime annotations

documents.title: Microservice Communication Patterns
documents.body: gRPC provides efficient binary protocol communication between services. REST over HTTPS suits public APIs. Message queues enable async service decoupling.
documents.description: Inter-service communication strategies for microservices

documents.title: Test-Driven Development
documents.body: TDD cycles through red green refactor steps. Writing tests first clarifies requirements. Rust test macros and tokio-test support async TDD workflows.
documents.description: Applying test-driven development in Rust projects

documents.title: Observability and Monitoring
documents.body: The three pillars of observability are metrics traces and logs. Prometheus scrapes metrics endpoints. Grafana visualises time-series metric data.
documents.description: Building observable systems with monitoring and alerting
